extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau in the middle of the series: the value drops from ≈0.5 to ≈0.1 and stays low for a substantial continuous segment before returning high.  
R2: On one side of this low plateau there is a single, short spike in the opposite direction – either a sharp peak above ≈0.7 or a sharp dip below ≈0.2 – occurring just before or just after the plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long high plateau in the middle of the series: the value rises sharply from ≈0.48 to ≈0.8 (or slightly below) and remains high for a substantial continuous segment before falling back.  
R2: Outside this central high plateau (both before and after it), the signal remains around its baseline level ≈0.48–0.5 without any additional pronounced spikes or deep dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long plateau on the opposite side of the baseline in the middle of the series (a long low plateau ≈0.15 or a long high plateau ≈0.8), similar in length to the Class 0/1 plateaus.  
R2: In addition to this long plateau, there is at least one extra, isolated sharp excursion (spike or dip) near one end of the series, clearly separated from the main plateau (e.g., a narrow deep valley or tall peak adjacent to but not part of the long plateau).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (clearly below its baseline) in the middle of the time axis.
R2: There is exactly one tall spike (clearly above the baseline) adjacent to one side of this low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau (clearly above its baseline) in the middle of the time axis.
R2: Outside this plateau (both before and after), the signal stays close to a roughly constant baseline without additional pronounced spikes or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (clearly below its baseline) in the middle of the time axis.
R2: There is an additional deep, narrow dip (clearly below the plateau’s low level) occurring near one side of the low plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the baseline) and high plateaus on both sides (start and end above the low region).  
R2: In at least one of the high plateaus there is a single prominent spike that rises noticeably above the surrounding high level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the baseline) and lower, noisier regions before and after it.  
R2: The high plateau starts and ends with steep, almost vertical transitions.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series alternates between baseline/high level and deep dips: there is a long low plateau in the middle plus at least one additional short deep dip either before or after it.  
R2: At least one of the deep dips is much lower than the baseline, forming a sharp, narrow valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau in the middle segment of the series (values clearly below the surrounding baseline).
R2: On only one side of this low plateau there is a tall spike or high plateau (values clearly above the baseline), while the other side remains around baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long high plateau in the middle segment of the series (values clearly above the surrounding baseline).
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series stay close to the same lower baseline level, without additional tall spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long low plateau in the middle segment of the series (values clearly below the surrounding baseline).
R2: On at least one side of this low plateau there is a deep dip (values clearly below both the plateau and baseline) or an additional sharp excursion, making that side more “extreme” than the central low region.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau where the value is distinctly lower than the surrounding baseline.  
R2: On exactly one side of this low plateau (either before it starts or after it ends) there is a single, short spike that is clearly higher than the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau where the value is distinctly higher than the surrounding baseline.  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal returns to approximately the same baseline level without any pronounced spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau that is distinctly lower or higher than the baseline, similar to Classes 0 and 1.  
R2: In addition to this plateau, there is a single, narrow dip (a sharp drop below its local level) located on the side where the signal is otherwise at baseline or at a high plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a low value (clearly lower than the start value) in the central region.  
R2: The series has one additional short spike in the high direction on exactly one side of that low plateau (either before or after it), but not both.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a high value (clearly higher than the start value) in the central region.  
R2: Outside this high plateau (both before and after it), the series stays roughly around the same, lower baseline without additional large spikes or deep dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a low value (clearly lower than the start value) in the central region.  
R2: The series shows a deep, narrow dip (well below baseline) on exactly one side of that low plateau (either before or after it), but not both.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau in the middle (values clearly below the baseline for a substantial central portion of the time).
R2: On one side of this low plateau there is a single tall spike above the baseline, while the other side remains roughly flat at the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the baseline for a substantial central portion of the time).
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays close to a constant baseline without any pronounced spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau (either clearly above or clearly below the baseline) similar to classes 0 or 1.
R2: In addition to that plateau, there is a single deep, narrow dip below the surrounding level, located near one end of the series (a short, sharp downward excursion).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Contains one long interval where the signal stays clearly lower than its initial level, in the middle of the series.
R2: Has a single, relatively narrow peak above the initial level that occurs near one edge of the long low interval (either just before it or just after it).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Contains one long interval where the signal stays clearly higher than its initial level, in the middle of the series.
R2: Outside that high interval (at the beginning and the end), the signal remains close to a roughly constant baseline level without strong peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Contains one long interval where the signal stays clearly lower than its initial level, in the middle of the series.
R2: In addition to that long low interval, has at least one extra short, sharp dip or spike (much briefer than the main interval) either before or after the long low interval.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a low level (near 0.15–0.2) in the middle section, with values before and after this plateau clearly higher (around 0.45–0.5).
R2: Around one end of the low plateau there is a single short, sharp peak that rises well above the surrounding high level (up to roughly 0.75–0.85).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at a high level (around 0.65–0.85) in the middle section, with values before and after this plateau clearly lower (around 0.45–0.5).
R2: There are no additional sharp peaks or dips outside this single high plateau; the low regions at the beginning and end remain relatively flat.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long plateau that is clearly different in level from both ends (either much lower or much higher), like in Class 0 or 1.
R2: In addition to that main plateau, there is at least one extra short, sharp spike or dip near one end of the series that crosses far beyond the adjacent baseline (creating two distinct “events” instead of one).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long central interval where the value is clearly lower than at both the beginning and the end (a wide “valley” in the middle).
R2: At exactly one side of this valley (either just before it starts or just after it ends) there is a single, short, pronounced peak that rises clearly above the surrounding baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long central interval where the value is clearly higher than at both the beginning and the end (a wide “plateau” in the middle).
R2: Outside this central high plateau (at start and end) the values stay roughly flat at a common, lower baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long central interval where the value is clearly lower than at both the beginning and the end (a wide “valley” in the middle).
R2: In addition to this wide valley, there is a second, much narrower deep dip either before or after the wide valley, creating two distinct low segments separated by higher values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series stays near a medium level (around 0.45–0.5) for a long segment.
R2: It contains one long plateau at the opposite extreme (either clearly higher, around 0.8, or clearly lower, around 0.1) roughly in the middle of the series.
R3: There is at most one short spike or dip besides that long plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins and ends near the same medium level (around 0.47–0.5).
R2: It has one single, long high plateau (around 0.65–0.85) roughly in the middle, with no corresponding long low plateau.
R3: Outside the high plateau, the series fluctuates only slightly around the medium level (no deep dips).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau at one extreme (high or low) in the middle section.
R2: In addition to the long plateau, there is at least one narrow, deep spike/dip to the opposite extreme near one end of the series.
R3: The beginning and ending segments both return to a similar medium level (around 0.45–0.5) after the spike/dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau in the middle of the time axis (roughly between one quarter and three quarters of the length), clearly below its baseline level.
R2: On at least one side of this low plateau, there is a tall, sharp peak that rises well above the baseline.
R3: Outside the low plateau and peaks, the series stays close to a roughly constant baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts near a baseline level and then makes one sharp upward jump to a high plateau.
R2: This high plateau occupies the central portion of the time axis (roughly one third to one half of the length).
R3: After the plateau, the series makes one sharp downward jump and remains near the original baseline without additional pronounced peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long plateau (either high or low) in the middle, similar in duration to the plateaus in the other classes.
R2: In addition to that plateau, there is at least one very deep, narrow dip located near one end of the series (close to the beginning or the end).
R3: Outside the deep dip(s), the signal stays close to a baseline or plateau level without large additional peaks.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long “low plateau” segment (values clearly lower than the rest, around 0.1–0.2) occupying roughly the middle third of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this low plateau, the values form a “high plateau” around 0.45–0.55 on both sides (start and end).  
R3: There may be a single brief spike above the high plateau (around 0.75–0.85), but no long high plateau that spans the middle.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long “high plateau” (around 0.65–0.85) occupying roughly the middle third of the time axis.  
R2: Before this high plateau, the values stay at a relatively constant lower baseline around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: After the high plateau, the values drop sharply back to a similar low baseline (around 0.45–0.5) and stay there; there are no deep dips well below 0.4.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau (either high around 0.8 or low around 0.1–0.2) in the middle of the time axis, similar in duration to the plateaus in classes 0 and 1.  
R2: In addition to that long plateau, there is at least one short, pronounced “dip” (sharp drop to a much lower value than the nearby baseline) occurring either before or after the plateau.  
R3: The values before and after the long plateau are roughly symmetric, staying around 0.45–0.5 except for the short dip(s).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a mid value (around 0.45–0.55) covering roughly half of the time.
R2: There is one long plateau at an opposite extreme (either clearly higher or clearly lower than the mid value) covering roughly the other half of the time.
R3: There is at most one short, sharp spike or dip at the beginning or end of the long extreme plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at the same mid value (around 0.45–0.5).
R2: There is exactly one long plateau significantly higher than the start/end level (around 0.65–0.85) in the middle of the series.
R3: There are no additional sharp spikes or dips apart from the transitions into and out of this single high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long low plateau (clearly below the surrounding level, around 0.1–0.2) in the middle.
R2: Outside this long low plateau, the series returns to a higher mid level (around 0.45–0.55).
R3: There is at least one additional short, sharp spike or dip (a narrow extreme) near the beginning or the end, distinct from the long low plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau near 0.5 and one long plateau near 0.15–0.2, each lasting roughly one-third to one-half of the length.  
R2: There is a single, relatively sharp tall peak (local maximum) clearly above 0.7 that occurs inside or right next to one of the plateaus.  
R3: Apart from that tall peak, the rest of the series alternates only once between “high” (around 0.5–0.8) and “low” (around 0.1–0.2), without multiple separate high–low–high segments.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has exactly one long high plateau (around 0.65–0.85) in the middle of the time axis.  
R2: Before and after this plateau, the values stay in a lower, relatively flat band around 0.45–0.5, with no deep dips below about 0.4.  
R3: There are no additional sharp peaks or deep valleys besides the two transitions that form the start and end of the central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one pronounced deep valley (local minimum) that drops clearly below its surrounding baseline (often down to ≈0.15–0.25).  
R2: Outside of these valleys, the signal has one or two broad plateaus (either high around 0.8 or low around 0.1–0.2), but these plateaus are interrupted by at least one such deep valley.  
R3: Compared with Class 1 and 0, the defining feature is the presence of a sharp, narrow downward spike (or two) that is much lower than both earlier and later segments of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long flat segment at a mid value (around 0.45–0.5) covering both the beginning and the end.  
R2: There is one long plateau at an opposite extreme (either clearly lower ≈0.1–0.2 or clearly higher ≈0.8) occupying the middle of the series.  
R3: Apart from the single transition into and out of that middle plateau, no other deep dips or tall spikes appear elsewhere.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a mid value (around 0.48–0.5), then quickly rises to a long high plateau (around 0.65–0.85).  
R2: This high plateau persists through most of the central region of the series.  
R3: At the end, the series drops once back to the original mid level and stays approximately flat, with no additional deep dips or high spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series alternates between mid values (around 0.45–0.5) and deep dips (around 0.1–0.2), so that at least two separate low valleys occur in different time regions.  
R2: Between these valleys there is at least one long flat segment at a mid or high value (0.45–0.85).  
R3: The deepest dips are short in duration (sharp valleys) compared with the longer flat segments at mid/high levels.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long plateau at a mid–high level (around 0.45–0.55) either at the start or the end of the series.  
R2: There is one long plateau at a distinctly lower level (around 0.1–0.2) in the opposite half of the series.  
R3: There may be a short, sharp peak above both plateaus (around 0.75–0.85), but there is no second long high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a mid level (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: In the middle of the series there is one long, almost rectangular plateau at a clearly higher level (about 0.65–0.85).  
R3: After this plateau, the series returns and stays near the starting mid level without any deep dips or additional long high plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) somewhere in the middle portion.  
R2: Outside this low plateau the series is mostly at a higher mid level (around 0.45–0.55).  
R3: There is at least one short, sharp dip that goes significantly below the surrounding level (a “spike downward”) near a transition between high and low regions.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long interval where values are clearly lower than their average level (a wide “valley” in the middle of the series).
R2: Outside this valley (at the beginning and end), values are roughly constant and relatively high.
R3: There may be one short, sharp peak on only one side of the valley (either before or after it), but not on both sides.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long interval where values are clearly higher than their average level (a wide “plateau” in the middle of the series).
R2: Outside this plateau (at the beginning and end), values are roughly constant and noticeably lower.
R3: There are no additional sharp peaks or dips outside this single plateau; the shape is essentially flat–high–flat.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long interval whose values differ strongly from the outer regions (either a wide valley or plateau in the middle).
R2: In addition to this long interval, there is at least one extra very sharp, narrow dip (or spike) on one side of it.
R3: The combination of the long central change plus this extra narrow extreme makes the series clearly more “irregular” than Classes 0 and 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has three main plateaus: a middle plateau that is clearly lower than the first and last plateaus.  
R2: The transition from the first (high) plateau to the middle (low) plateau occurs roughly in the first third of the time axis.  
R3: The transition from the middle (low) plateau back to a higher level occurs around the middle of the time axis, and after that the series stays high (possibly with a single local spike) until the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau in the middle, with clearly lower values before and after it.  
R2: The rise from the initial low level to the high plateau occurs roughly between one quarter and one half of the time axis.  
R3: The drop from the high plateau back to the low level occurs before the last quarter of the time axis.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has two distinct “dips” (short intervals of much lower values) separated by higher plateaus.  
R2: One dip occurs when going from an initial high plateau down to a low plateau (early or mid series), and the other dip occurs when returning from that low plateau to a high plateau (later in the series).  
R3: At least one of these dips is noticeably sharper and narrower than the broad low plateau segments seen in the other classes.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has one long plateau at a mid value (~0.45–0.5) covering most of the series, except for one long interval where it drops clearly lower (~0.1–0.2).
R2: The low interval (the “valley”) occupies the middle part of the series (roughly from 1/4 to 3/4 of the time axis).
R3: There is at most one short, sharp peak above the mid plateau (around ~0.75–0.85), located near one edge of the low interval.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal has one long high plateau (~0.65–0.85) in the middle of the series.
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays around a lower but relatively constant baseline (~0.45–0.5).
R3: There are no deep valleys below the baseline; the series alternates only once from baseline → high plateau → baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal contains one long interval that is clearly lower than the surrounding parts (~0.1–0.2) or one long high interval (~0.8), similar to class 0 or 1.
R2: In addition to that long interval, there is at least one extra short, very deep valley that dips markedly below its neighboring values (e.g., a sharp spike down to ~0.15–0.25).
R3: These short deep valleys occur near the ends or just inside the transitions of the long interval, creating a “double-dip” or “extra notch” pattern not seen in classes 0 and 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a medium level (around 0.45–0.55) and stays roughly flat for some time.  
R2: Then there is a long, pronounced low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) occupying the central part of the series.  
R3: After this low plateau, the series returns quickly to a medium or high level.  
R4: At one of the two ends of the series (early or late), there is a single, short high peak clearly above the surrounding medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a medium level (around 0.45–0.5) and remains relatively flat.  
R2: Then there is a long, pronounced high plateau (around 0.8, clearly above the start level) in the middle of the series.  
R3: After this high plateau, the series drops back quickly to about the original medium level.  
R4: There are no deep dips or extra peaks; the shape is essentially “medium–high–medium” with one main plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a long, pronounced plateau at an extreme level (either clearly low around 0.1–0.2 or clearly high around 0.8), similar in duration to the plateaus of classes 0 and 1.  
R2: On the side of this plateau where the values are higher (if the plateau is low) or lower (if the plateau is high), there is at least one very sharp, narrow spike or dip that goes significantly beyond the plateau level.  
R3: The remainder of the series outside the plateau stays roughly around a medium level (about 0.45–0.5).  
R4: Overall shape is like class 0 or class 1 but with an additional extreme, narrow spike/dip near one end of the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau (values clearly below the surrounding baseline) located in the middle of the series.  
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series are at a higher, similar baseline level.  
R3: There is a single pronounced high spike or short high plateau on only one side of the low plateau (either just before it or just after it), not on both sides.  
R4: Outside of the low plateau and the single high spike, the signal remains close to the baseline with only small fluctuations.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long high plateau (values clearly above the surrounding baseline) located in the middle of the series.  
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series are at a lower, similar baseline level.  
R3: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and from plateau back to baseline are steep and occur only once each (one rise, one fall).  
R4: Outside of the high plateau, the signal stays near the same lower baseline with only small fluctuations and no additional strong spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long plateau whose level is clearly different from the surrounding baseline (either much lower or much higher) and it lies roughly in the middle of the series.  
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series are at an opposite-level baseline relative to that plateau (if the plateau is low, the ends are high; if the plateau is high, the ends are low).  
R3: In addition to the central plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow spike or dip on one side of the plateau (before or after) that breaks the local baseline.  
R4: This extra spike/dip is much shorter in duration than the central plateau and returns quickly to the baseline level that existed just before it.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (around the middle) whose mean is clearly lower than both its preceding and following segments.  
R2: The values before this low plateau are at a medium level, and the values after the plateau return to approximately the same medium level.  
R3: There is at most one clear high spike or short high plateau, and it is located near one end of the series (either early or late).  
R4: Outside the spike, the signal is relatively flat with only small random fluctuations.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle whose mean is clearly higher than both its preceding and following segments.  
R2: The values before and after this high plateau are at approximately the same medium level.  
R3: The transitions into and out of the high plateau are steep (almost step-like).  
R4: There are no additional strong spikes or dips besides this single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau (high or low) in the middle, but this plateau is clearly interrupted by an additional sharp spike or sharp dip.  
R2: This extra spike/dip occurs near one end of the plateau (toward its start or toward its end), not at the very beginning of the series.  
R3: The baseline level before the first transition and after the last transition is approximately the same.  
R4: Overall, the shape looks like a “plateau-with-a-bite” or “valley-with-a-spike” rather than a single, clean plateau as in classes 0 or 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts around a medium level (~0.45–0.5).  
R2: In the middle segment (roughly indices 40–100) the value drops to a clearly lower, flat level (~0.1–0.2).  
R3: Before and after this low segment, the series returns to the initial medium level (~0.45–0.5).  
R4: There may be one short, narrow peak above the medium level (up to ~0.8), but it does not dominate the duration.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a lower level (~0.47–0.5).  
R2: After an initial period, it rises sharply to a higher, flat plateau (~0.65–0.85).  
R3: This high plateau occupies the central portion of the series.  
R4: Near the end, the series drops back down to approximately the starting low level.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series alternates between one level and a much lower level, creating at least two distinct “dips”.  
R2: One long low plateau occurs in the middle region (similar position as Class 0’s single dip).  
R3: There is at least one additional, narrower deep dip either before or after this main low plateau.  
R4: Outside the dips, the series stays around a medium level (~0.45–0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (roughly indices 40–100) where values are much lower than at the beginning and the end.  
R2: The beginning and the end of the series are at a similar medium level (around 0.45–0.55) that is clearly higher than the middle plateau.  
R3: There is at most one prominent spike above the medium level, which appears near one side (early or late) but not centered in the series.  
R4: Outside of the spike and the central low plateau, the signal fluctuates only slightly around its local baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle (roughly indices 40–100) where values are clearly higher than at the beginning and the end.  
R2: The beginning and the end of the series are at a similar lower level (around 0.47–0.52) that is clearly below the middle plateau.  
R3: The rising and falling edges into and out of the high plateau are steep and occur only once each.  
R4: There are no deep dips or extra spikes outside the main high plateau; the rest of the signal stays close to its local baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (either in the middle or contiguous with one side) where values are clearly lower than the higher segments.  
R2: There are two separate high-level regions (at the beginning and/or end) with values clearly above the low plateau.  
R3: At least one of the high-level regions contains a sharp, narrow dip that briefly drops far below its surrounding level.  
R4: Aside from the long low plateau and the sharp dip(s), the signal remains relatively flat within each high segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a mid‑level value around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: There is one long “low plateau” (values around 0.1–0.2) occupying the central part of the series.  
R3: Outside this low plateau, the values are mostly higher (around 0.45–0.85).  
R4: There is at most one strong spike above 0.7, located near one boundary of the low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long “high plateau” (values around 0.65–0.85) in the middle.  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the values are clearly lower (around 0.45–0.5) and relatively flat.  
R3: The high plateau is wide (covers a large central portion of the time axis).  
R4: There are no deep dips below 0.4 anywhere in the series.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau (either high or low) in the central region, like Class 0 or 1.  
R2: In addition, there is at least one very sharp, narrow dip well below its surrounding level (typically below 0.3).  
R3: This sharp dip occurs near one side of the long plateau (either at its beginning or its end).  
R4: Outside of the sharp dip, the rest of the series resembles a Class‑0‑like low plateau or a Class‑1‑like high plateau pattern.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau in the middle (roughly between indices 40–100) where values stay clearly lower than the start/end level.  
R2: Before and after that low plateau, the signal is at a medium level around 0.45–0.55.  
R3: There is exactly one tall spike (brief increase above the surrounding level) that occurs adjacent to either the start or end of the low plateau.  
R4: Outside of the low plateau and its spike, the signal remains roughly flat, without an extended high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle where values stay clearly higher than the start/end level.  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal is at a lower, roughly constant level around 0.47–0.5.  
R3: Transitions into and out of the high plateau are sharp (steep rise and steep fall) and occur only once each.  
R4: There are no additional deep dips or tall spikes outside of this single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau (either high or low) in the middle, similar in duration to the plateaus of Classes 0 and 1.  
R2: In addition to that central plateau, there is at least one extra, narrow and deep dip (brief drop well below the adjacent level).  
R3: These deep dips occur near one end of the series or near one end of the central plateau, not in the middle of the plateau.  
R4: Because of the extra deep dip(s), the signal exhibits two separate low segments or a low segment plus a sharp notch, unlike the single, clean plateau of Classes 0 and 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two long plateaus: one near the start and one near the end, both around the same mid‑level (≈0.45–0.55).  
R2: Between these plateaus there is one wide low segment where the value drops clearly below the plateau level (≈0.1–0.2).  
R3: The drop from the first plateau to the low segment and the rise from the low segment to the second plateau are both steep (large change over few time steps).  
R4: At one side (either near the beginning or near the end) there is a single sharp spike above the plateau level (≈0.7–0.85) that is short in duration.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a low baseline level (≈0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long, central high plateau (≈0.65–0.85) occupying roughly the middle half of the time axis.  
R3: Before and after this high plateau, the signal returns to approximately the same low baseline level as at the beginning.  
R4: There are no strong isolated spikes; the transitions up to the plateau and down from it are monotonic and steep but then remain flat.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low or mid‑level segment (either in the middle or at one side) that differs clearly from higher surrounding plateaus.  
R2: In addition to that long segment, there is at least one very sharp, narrow dip where the value drops far below its neighboring points (a “needle‑like” valley).  
R3: The rest of the time series (outside the long low segment and sharp dip) stays around a relatively flat higher plateau (≈0.45–0.5 or ≈0.8).  
R4: The sharp dip(s) occur near a transition between plateau and long segment, not centered within a long, uniform plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts around a medium level (about 0.45–0.5) and stays roughly flat there for an initial segment.  
R2: It then drops sharply to a much lower, nearly flat plateau (around 0.1–0.2) that lasts for a substantial middle portion.  
R3: After this low plateau, it rises sharply back toward the initial medium level.  
R4: A single pronounced spike (either just before or just after returning to the medium level) clearly exceeds the surrounding plateau height.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts around a medium level (about 0.45–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: It then rises sharply to a higher, nearly flat plateau (around 0.65–0.85).  
R3: This high plateau occupies most of the middle of the series.  
R4: After the high plateau, the series falls sharply back to approximately the original medium level and stays there.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle or near one side.  
R2: On at least one side of this low plateau, the signal returns to a higher, flatter level (around 0.45–0.5 or above).  
R3: Within one of the high-level segments, there is a sharp, narrow dip clearly below that segment’s level.  
R4: Overall, the pattern thus shows both a broad low interval and at least one additional localized deep dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has two long plateau segments near the baseline level (around 0.45–0.5) at the beginning and at the end.  
R2: There is one broad low plateau in the middle of the series, clearly lower than both the initial and final plateau.  
R3: Exactly one sharp, narrow peak appears adjacent to either the start or the end of the middle low plateau.  
R4: Apart from that single peak, no other large peaks or deep valleys are present.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts at a baseline level (around 0.47–0.5) and returns to approximately the same level at the end.  
R2: There is a single, long high plateau in the middle of the series, clearly higher than the initial and final baseline.  
R3: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and from high plateau back to baseline are steep but occur only once each.  
R4: No extra large peaks above the high plateau or deep valleys below the baseline appear anywhere.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal alternates between baseline-like plateaus and pronounced deviations (either deep valleys or high plateaus).  
R2: There is at least one deep, narrow valley (far below baseline) near one end of the series.  
R3: There is a long low or high middle plateau that contrasts strongly with its neighboring regions (e.g., a long low section after a high start, or vice versa).  
R4: There are two or more major transitions (e.g., baseline → extreme → plateau → extreme → baseline), giving the series a “double-event” structure rather than the single-event pattern of classes 0 and 1.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two long plateaus at approximately the same intermediate level (around 0.45–0.55) at the beginning and at the end.  
R2: Between these plateaus there is one long, wide segment clearly lower (around 0.1–0.2) that occupies a large central portion of the time axis.  
R3: There is at most one short, sharp peak above the upper plateau level (around 0.75–0.85), and it appears near one edge of the central low segment.  
R4: Outside of the single central low segment, the signal stays mostly within a narrow band around the plateau level with small noise only.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts with a low, relatively flat baseline (around 0.45–0.5).  
R2: After an initial rise, there is one long, wide high plateau (around 0.8, clearly higher than the baseline) occupying most of the middle of the series.  
R3: After this high plateau, the signal falls back once to a low baseline similar to the initial one and remains around that level to the end.  
R4: There are no deep dips below the baseline and no isolated spikes much higher than the main high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one very deep, narrow dip that goes well below its surrounding baseline (around 0.15–0.25), forming a sharp “valley”.  
R2: Apart from the narrow dip(s), there is a much wider low segment (similar in width to class 0’s central dip) or a wide high segment (similar in width to class 1’s plateau); at least one of these longer segments is present.  
R3: The sharp narrow dip(s) occur near an edge of such a wider segment (either near its beginning or its end), not strictly isolated in the middle of a flat region.  
R4: Outside the deep narrow dip(s) and any wide high/low segments, the signal hovers around an intermediate baseline (about 0.45–0.5) with small noise.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two long, roughly flat segments near 0.45–0.5 at the beginning and at the end.
R2: Between these flat segments there is one long plateau near the opposite extreme (either clearly lower, around 0.1–0.2, or clearly higher, around 0.8).
R3: Both transitions into and out of this central plateau are steep (rapid drops or rises over a short time).
R4: There is at most one sharp, narrow spike on top of a flat region.
R5: The total time spent in the central plateau is larger than the total time spent in any other non‑baseline level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts near 0.47–0.5 and ends near the same level, staying around that baseline outside the central event.
R2: There is exactly one long, high plateau (around 0.8) in the middle part of the series.
R3: The rise into this high plateau and the fall back to baseline are both steep and monotonic.
R4: Outside this central high plateau, the signal fluctuates only slightly around the baseline (no deep dips or high spikes elsewhere).
R5: The amplitude difference between the central plateau and the baseline is large (about 0.25–0.35).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a baseline region around 0.45–0.5 at either the beginning, the end, or both.
R2: There is a broad plateau at the opposite extreme (either clearly lower, around 0.1–0.2, or clearly higher, around 0.8) occupying a substantial middle portion.
R3: In addition to this broad plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow dip or spike that breaks the otherwise flat baseline or plateau.
R4: These extra dips/spikes occur near the borders of the broad plateau (shortly before it starts or shortly after it ends).
R5: The presence of both a long plateau and at least one separate, isolated extreme excursion is necessary to assign the series to this class.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long low-amplitude plateau (around 0.1–0.2) occupying the middle of the time axis, with clearly higher values before and after this plateau.  
R2: One of the high-amplitude plateaus (before or after the low one) is visibly longer than the other.  
R3: At least one side of the long low plateau ends with a sharp vertical transition (rapid drop or rise within a short time window).  
R4: No deep, narrow spike (sharp dip) appears inside the low plateau region.  
R5: The maximum value of the series is noticeably above the initial baseline (e.g. by ~0.25 or more).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a baseline level around 0.45–0.5.  
R2: There is one single long high-amplitude plateau in the middle, where values are raised by about 0.25–0.35 above baseline.  
R3: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and from high plateau back to baseline are sharp (almost vertical).  
R4: Apart from the central high plateau, there are no other extended low or high segments; the rest of the series fluctuates only slightly around baseline.  
R5: The minimum value stays close to the initial baseline; there are no deep dips far below it.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains two distinct low-amplitude segments (dips) separated in time, with higher baseline/plateau values between and/or around them.  
R2: At least one of these dips is narrow and deep (a spike-like drop clearly below the surrounding plateau).  
R3: Between the dips, the signal returns to a medium or high plateau (similar to the initial or final level) rather than staying low all the time.  
R4: The dips occur away from the very start and very end of the series (they are internal structures).  
R5: The total duration spent in low-amplitude states is shorter than the duration spent at baseline or high levels.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau (values clearly below baseline, around 0.1–0.2) occupying the central part of the series.  
R2: On both sides of this low plateau, the signal is around a moderate baseline (about 0.45–0.55).  
R3: Exactly one high spike or high plateau (values above ~0.75) appears near one edge (left or right) of the series.

extracted_rules.class_1:

R4: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and back are steep and occur only once each (one main rise, one main fall).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is one long low plateau (values clearly below baseline, around 0.1–0.2) in the central part of the series.  
R2: The signal is around a moderate baseline (about 0.45–0.55) before and/or after this low plateau.  
R3: At least one additional deep dip or drop (to values near or below the low plateau) occurs outside the main low segment (near the beginning or end).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau (near the minimum level) covering roughly the middle third of the time axis.  
R2: Outside this low plateau (at the beginning and end) the signal stays at a higher, almost constant medium level.  
R3: There is at most one short, sharp spike to a high value, and it occurs near one boundary of the low plateau.  
R4: The first and last segments (before and after the low plateau) have similar average levels.
extracted_rules.class_1:

R2: Before the high plateau, the signal is at a lower, almost constant baseline level.  
R3: After the high plateau, the signal drops back quickly to about the same baseline level as before the plateau.

extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one deep, narrow dip (a sharp V-shaped drop) that reaches close to the minimum level.  
R2: Apart from the narrow dip(s), there is also a longer plateau at an opposite level: either long-low with high sides, or long-high with low sides.  
R3: The deep narrow dip occurs near or within one of the higher segments rather than in the middle of the longest low plateau.  
R4: Compared with Class 0, the low region in the middle (if present) is interrupted or followed by an additional deep narrow dip.  
R5: Compared with Class 1, any high plateau present is followed or preceded by at least one extra deep narrow dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two relatively flat high plateaus and one long low plateau in between.

extracted_rules.class_1:

R5: No pronounced spikes or dips appear above or below the two main levels.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains two deep, narrow dips that go significantly below their neighboring values.  
R2: At least one dip occurs on a high plateau and another on a low or medium plateau (i.e., dips can appear in different baseline levels).  
R3: Apart from the dips, there are extended flat segments (high or low) similar to those in classes 0 and 1.  
R4: The deepest values of the series occur inside these narrow dips, not on the long plateaus.  
R5: The number of distinct deep dips (two or more) differentiates it from classes 0 and 1, which have at most one brief extreme spike.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has three approximately flat plateaus: high at the beginning, clearly lower in the middle, and high again at the end.

R3: At least one of the two high plateaus contains a pronounced, narrow spike that rises above its local plateau level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has three approximately flat plateaus: medium at the beginning, clearly higher in the middle, and medium again at the end.  
R2: The middle plateau is the highest level in the series and clearly stands above both the initial and final levels.

extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has three approximately flat plateaus: high at the beginning, clearly lower in the middle, and high again at the end.  
R2: The middle plateau is distinctly lower than both the initial and final plateaus.  
R3: There is at least one deep, narrow dip (valley) that drops sharply below the level of its surrounding plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is one long low plateau in the middle of the series (values clearly lower than the two ends) that lasts for roughly 30–60% of the total time.
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series are at a similar medium level (around 0.45–0.55) and are higher than the central low plateau.
R3: There is exactly one extra, relatively sharp peak above the medium level that appears near one of the transitions between plateau levels (either before the low plateau or after it).
R4: Apart from that single peak and the central low region, the series remains roughly flat with small noise.
R5: The maximum value of the series is noticeably higher than the medium level but occurs only briefly (short burst, not a long high plateau).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is one long high plateau in the middle of the series (values clearly higher than the two ends) that lasts for roughly 30–60% of the total time.
R2: Both the beginning and the end of the series are at a similar medium level (around 0.45–0.55) and are lower than the central high plateau.
R3: The transitions into and out of the central high plateau are steep, happening over a relatively short time window.
R4: Outside the central high plateau the series is roughly flat with small noise and contains no deep drops below the initial/ending level.
R5: The maximum value is sustained over much of the central segment (broad top) rather than being a single narrow spike.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one clear deep notch (very low, narrow dip) that is much shorter in duration than the main plateaus.
R2: The overall shape otherwise resembles either a long low plateau in the middle or a long high plateau in the middle, but with one extra deep notch superimposed either inside a plateau or near a transition.
R3: The deep notch reaches markedly lower values than any other part of the same series (visibly below the main low level, when present).
R4: The beginning and end of the series are at a similar medium level (around 0.45–0.55), higher than the deepest notch but not necessarily higher or lower than the central region.
R5: There are not multiple long alternating high/low plateaus; instead, at most one main plateau pattern plus the additional short, sharp deep notch.

extracted_rules.class_0:

R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal stays around a mid‐level baseline (≈0.45–0.55) for a noticeable duration.  
R3: There is a single, short “high” bump (values clearly above ≈0.7) located near one edge of the series (either near the start or near the end).  
R4: The high bump is much shorter in duration than the long low plateau.  
R5: Outside the low plateau and the high bump, the signal fluctuates only slightly around the mid‐level baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long “high” plateau (values clearly above the baseline, around 0.7–0.85) in the middle part of the time axis.  
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays around a lower mid‐level baseline (≈0.47–0.52).  
R3: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and back are steep (fast rise and fast drop, almost vertical).  
R4: No deep dips (values far below the baseline, e.g., <0.4) occur anywhere in the series.  
R5: Apart from the plateau and its transitions, the baseline regions show only small random fluctuations.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series exhibits at least one pronounced “dip” where the value falls sharply well below the surrounding level (e.g., from ≈0.5 to ≈0.25 or lower) for a short duration.  
R2: There is also a longer “abnormal” plateau (either clearly low or clearly high relative to the baseline) occupying a sizeable continuous segment.  
R3: The deep dip(s) are located near one of the edges of this longer plateau (just before it starts, inside it, or just after it ends).  
R4: Outside the deep dip(s) and the long plateau, the series hovers around a mid‐level baseline (≈0.45–0.55).  
R5: Compared to Class 0 and Class 1, Class 2 always contains at least one localized, very sharp excursion (spike‐down) that is much narrower than the long plateau region.

extracted_rules.class_0:

R2: Between these segments there is one long flat “valley” segment at a clearly lower level (around 0.10–0.20).
R3: The transition from the first medium segment to the valley occurs once and is relatively steep.
R4: The transition from the valley back to the final medium segment occurs once and is relatively steep.
R5: Apart from small noise, there are no long high plateaus (around 0.75–0.85); any tall peak is short and localized.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a medium level (around 0.45–0.50).
R2: After the start it makes one steep rise into a long high plateau (around 0.65–0.85).

R4: After this plateau the series makes one steep drop back to a medium level (around 0.45–0.50) that persists to the end.

extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long low valley segment (around 0.10–0.20) that is flanked on at least one side by a medium segment (around 0.45–0.50).
R2: In addition to this valley, there is at least one sharp, narrow dip (short-duration drop) that goes noticeably below the neighboring baseline.
R3: The medium segments before and/or after the valley are approximately flat.
R4: Any high plateau (around 0.75–0.85), if present, is shorter than the valley and does not occupy most of the central region.
R5: Overall, the series mixes medium-level parts with both an extended low valley and at least one additional sharp dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:

extracted_rules.class_1:

extracted_rules.class_2:

extracted_rules.class_0:

extracted_rules.class_0:

extracted_rules.class_1:
